name: Botswana
avoid: November-March rains can flood tracks, raise delta water unpredictably, and make self-drive sections slow
Safari charters usually enforce soft-bag luggage limits, so rigid suitcases create problems on Maun-Delta sectors
Night driving between Maun, Nata, and Kasane is risky because of livestock and wildlife on the highway
stopoverNote: Many India itineraries route via Johannesburg or Addis Ababa; if budget allows, use an open-jaw into Maun or Kasane so you do not lose a full day backtracking across northern Botswana.

cities.name: Gaborone
cities.notes: Capital-city reset with Three Dikgosi Monument, the National Museum district, and Mokolodi Nature Reserve before flying north

cities.name: Maun
cities.notes: Botswana's safari gateway for Okavango Delta mokoro trips, Moremi drives, scenic flights, and lodge-charter connections

cities.name: Chobe National Park
cities.notes: Kasane-based river-and-savanna safari hub known for elephant herds, Chobe River boat cruises, and easy park access

cities.name: Makgadikgadi Pans
cities.notes: Immense salt-pan landscapes with baobabs, meerkats, quad-bike desert routes, and Boteti River wildlife edges

cities.name: Nata
cities.notes: Roadside gateway to Sua Pan and Nata Bird Sanctuary, especially rewarding for flamingos after seasonal water fills

itinerary.sim: Mascom is the safest all-round SIM for this route, with Orange Botswana a workable second option in towns; expect roughly ₹620–₹1,550 for a starter pack or eSIM with enough data for maps, lodge WhatsApp, and flight confirmations, but coverage still drops deep in the Delta and inside parts of Chobe.

days.theme: A slow pan-edge transit day with flamingos, open skies, and one of Botswana's easiest wildlife stopovers

days.theme: Baobabs, salt-pan scale, meerkats, and the most otherworldly finish in Botswana

activities.name: Nxai Pan or Makgadikgadi National Park international entry fee
Park vehicle fee for day access
Guided meerkat habituation walk near Gweta
Quad-bike or 4x4 pans excursion with baobab sunset stop
Makgadikgadi salt-pan transfer from Nata or Gweta

connections.method: Air Botswana is the cleanest move for this itinerary; long-distance buses exist, but the domestic flight protects almost a full travel day
Self-drive 4x4 via Nata is the common independent option, while lodge-linked air charters or road transfers are faster but far more expensive
Drive south on the A33 in a rented 4x4 or private transfer; daytime travel is strongly preferred because of animals on the road
Continue by 4x4 toward Gweta, Planet Baobab, or a pans camp; some lodges bundle the last sandy approach in their excursion rate
Either drive the long southern return in your rental or backtrack to Maun and connect onward by domestic flight depending on where your international ticket ends

extras: May to October is the sweet spot for this exact loop: drier tracks, easier wildlife sightings around water, and more reliable road conditions for the Maun-Chobe-Nata self-drive sections.
Park fees in Botswana are usually charged per person per day, and private vehicles pay separately, so self-drive days become noticeably pricier once you stack gate fees with fuel and guide costs.
Delta charters almost always require soft bags around 15kg, so repack before the Maun flight if you are carrying hard-shell luggage from India.
Kasane and Maun are the easiest places to top up fuel, withdraw cash, and sort SIM issues; once you are in the pans or on a lodge-linked Delta segment, logistics get much less flexible.
Nata Bird Sanctuary is best just after enough water has collected to draw flamingos, but even in drier months the sunset views over Sua Pan still justify the stop on the Chobe-Maun corridor.
If you want the highest-value splurge, spend it on one Maun-based Delta activity day or a proper Chobe boat safari rather than upgrading every hotel on the route.
